Motion To Approve And Confirm Sale Of Real Property, Or, In The Alternative, Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Aug-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Russell Rowen 415-448-5000 of Thompson, Welch, Soroko & Gilbert LLP appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Hatsumi Takahashi, real estate professional hired by both plaintiff & defendant.
Mieko Saito 415-231-4528plaintiff in pro per.
Japanese Interpreter Eri Minoura-certified court interpreter, oath on file.

Hearing held. Real estate professional informs the court and defendant of sale price. The court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MASAHIRO SEKINE Motion To Approve And Confirm Sale Of Real Property, Or, In The Alternative, Appointing Receiver. The court confirms and apporoves sale. Plaintiff's request for uneven distribution is denied without prejudice. Defendant is not to interfere with sale or contact buyers. Prevailing party to prepare order.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: L. Young; Not reported. (D501)

Motion To Approve And Confirm Sale Of Real Property, Or, In The Alternative, Appointing Receiver
Off Calendar Jul-03-2023 Continued To Aug-08-23 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Russell F. Rowen; Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP; 415-450-0295; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Masahiro Sekine.
Mieko Saito; 415-231-4528; defendant, appearing in pro per.

Eri Minoura, Certified Japanese Interpreter present with an oath on file.

Hearing held. The court expresses concern over the fact that the current sale price is substantially lower than the appraisal value. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO APPROVE AND CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, APPOINTING RECEIVER is continued to August 8 2023 at 2:00pm in Department 501. Plaintiff's counsel to submit the following two orders to the court for execution forthwith: 1) Order stating that the court will confirm sale after reasonable efforts have been made by the broker, and 2) Order that the court has assumed jurisdiction over the sale of the property, and has not, and does not, approve of the sale as set forth in the addendum.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

No appearances by counsel or defendant. The Court thanks and excuses the Interpreter who appeared to assist in the Japanese language and notes it has received a declaration from the Defendant in response to this motion. It is not clear whether Defendant opposes the motion. The tentative ruling indicated that a hearing is required. There being no appearances from the defendant or the moving party, the Court takes the motion off calendar. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Noticeof Motion And Motion To Enforce The Settlement
Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Eric D. McFarland; Thompson Welch Soroko & Gilbert LLP; 415-450-0295; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Masahiro Sekine.
Mieko Saito; 415-231-4528; defendant, appearing without their counsel of record on their own behalf.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling, and provides a filed/endorsed copy of the Proof of Service of their moving papers. The proof of service was filed August 24, 2022 and improperly listed as a "Notice of Motion" on the court's register of actions. Defendant's legal representation is discussed, and the court informs defendant that they are still represented in the eyes of the law, as no substitution of attorney is on file. The court further explains to defendant that the court cannot take evidence or argument from defendant without their attorney present. Plaintiff states that defendant still occupies the premises, will not return the keys, has not cooperated in the sale of the property, and has asked for $800,000.00 in exchange for returning the keys to the premises. Defendant states she does not live at the property, but has not returned the keys. Defendant states that a realtor has asked that she replace the carpet and paint, and is planning on doing so. Defendant admits that she has noted signed any papers for sale of the property. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
